title: Dependency Remediation Policy
body:
## Purpose
This policy defines the required timelines and processes for remediating vulnerabilities in third-party dependencies used in production systems.

## Patching SLAs
- Critical severity vulnerabilities (CVSS >= 9.0): must be patched within 72 hours of identification.
- High severity (CVSS 7.0-8.9): must be patched within 1 week.
- Medium severity (CVSS 4.0-6.9): must be patched within 30 days.
- Low severity (CVSS < 4.0): should be patched in the next regular release cycle or within 90 days.

## Risk Acceptance Criteria
If a patch cannot be applied due to technical constraints, a risk acceptance request must be submitted to the Security Lead. The request must include:
- Description of the vulnerability and its impact.
- Reason patch cannot be applied (e.g., breaking changes, unavailable fix).
- Proposed compensating controls.
- Expiration date for the acceptance (maximum 90 days).
The Security Lead will review and either approve, reject, or request additional mitigations. All acceptances are logged in the risk register.

## Exceptions
Exceptions to these timelines require VP of Engineering approval.
